generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined temperature sensor integrated into BIM model for spatial alignment with physical structure, providing operational parameters for building management with high-frequency sampling for real-time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance MotionDetector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined motion detector integrated into BIM model for occupancy monitoring, supporting real-time data exchange with sub-second latency requirements."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined environmental monitor (e.g., air quality, humidity) integrated into BIM model for dynamic building management, ensuring continuous synchronization with physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyUsageSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined energy usage sensor integrated into BIM model for real-time energy consumption monitoring, supporting predictive analytics and optimization services."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings transmitted from TemperatureSensor with optimized data transmission rates for continuous synchronization between physical and digital systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data transmitted from MotionDetector with sub-second latency for dynamic building management and space utilization optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotionDetector
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental data (air quality, humidity) transmitted from EnvironmentalMonitor with high-frequency sampling for operational parameter validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalMonitor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption data transmitted from EnergyUsageSensor with optimized protocols for predictive analytics and energy efficiency services."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyUsageSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric representation of the physical building structure derived from BIM models, serving as the foundational semantic layer for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler
]

instance BcsBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral model integrating Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) defined by Building Control Engineers within BIM, enabling real-time operational interactions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BcsIntegrationEnabler
]

instance AnalyticalRuntimeModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model generated via Model-Driven Engineering principles to transform enriched BIM models into deployable Digital Twin artifacts with real-time synchronization capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MdeTransformationEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed temperature data from TemperatureDataTransmitted, validated for quality and normalized for integration into predictive analytics and control services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed occupancy data from OccupancyDataTransmitted, used for space utilization optimization and automated control logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental data (air quality, humidity) from EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, supporting real-time monitoring and adaptive control services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler, ImmersiveVisualizationEnabler
]

instance EnergyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed energy consumption data from EnergyDataTransmitted, enabling energy optimization analytics and predictive maintenance services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler, EnergyOptimizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimToDtTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework component that extracts structured representations from BIM models enriched with BCS metadata, automating the transformation into deployable Digital Twin artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, DeploymentManagementService
]

instance BcsIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized extension point for integrating Building Control System components (sensors, actuators) into the Digital Twin framework, ensuring semantic interoperability with BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance MdeTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering principles applied to generate analytical models from enriched BIM data, supporting runtime synchronization and custom service integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceExtensionService
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) ensuring continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their Digital Twins with sub-second latency."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ImmersiveVisualizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics modules integrated via custom plugins to analyze historical and real-time data for optimization recommendations (e.g., energy consumption patterns, fault prediction)."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyOptimizationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "WebGL-based 3D rendering engine providing interactive visualization interfaces for Facility Managers, overlaying live sensor data onto BIM models for spatial-aware monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tooling for DT Engineers to build and integrate domain-specific plugins (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization) using standardized APIs and microservice architectures."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceExtensionService
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Building Control Engineers to define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within BIM, creating semantic-rich structured representations for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationInsight, AutomatedExtractionAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional, continuous data exchange service ensuring sub-second latency synchronization between physical building systems and their Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, OperationalAlertAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control logic service implementing automated adjustments to actuators (e.g., dampers, valves) based on predictive analytics or predefined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlRecommendationInsight, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance CustomServiceExtensionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular service enabling runtime integration of third-party services (e.g., IoT gateways, AI/ML models) for advanced functionalities like predictive maintenance or anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides ServiceExtensionInsight, PluginIntegrationAction
]

instance DeploymentManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated service generating deployable Digital Twin artifacts (e.g., microservices, API endpoints) from enriched BIM models using Model-Driven Engineering principles."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentArtifactInsight, ArtifactGenerationAction
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization service providing Facility Managers with spatial-aware monitoring of system states through live sensor data overlays on BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight, InteractiveControlAction
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Energy efficiency optimization service analyzing real-time and historical energy data to recommend consumption patterns and fault detection strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyInsight, OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service detecting anomalies in building systems through real-time data analysis and historical pattern recognition."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultInsight, MaintenanceAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DeviceConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured representation of Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) with metadata for calibration, communication protocols, and spatial alignment."
]

]

]

]

instance ControlRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized control recommendations derived from predictive analytics or predefined operational thresholds for building systems."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ServiceExtensionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Catalog of available custom services and plugins for extending Digital Twin functionality beyond core operational capabilities."
]

instance PluginIntegrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual or automated integration of third-party services into the Digital Twin framework via standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeploymentArtifactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated deployable artifacts including microservices, API endpoints, and configuration files for Digital Twin operational deployment."
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of building systems with live sensor data overlays for spatial-aware monitoring."
]

]

instance EnergyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzed energy consumption patterns and optimization opportunities derived from real-time and historical data."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for energy efficiency improvements based on predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected anomalies and potential faults in building systems through real-time data analysis."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined HVAC system actuator integrated into BIM model for dynamic environmental control, supporting automated adjustments based on real-time sensor data."
]

instance LightingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined lighting controller integrated into BIM model for occupancy-based automation, enabling remote control via Digital Twin interfaces."
]

instance SecuritySystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineer-defined security system actuator integrated into BIM model for real-time monitoring and control of access points and surveillance systems."
]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the DT framework facilitates **real-time control commands** from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed via a **bidirectional, validated command pipeline**. Control flows include **actuation commands** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control, equipment activation) and **parameter updates** (e.g., setpoints, operational thresholds) formatted in **standardized message structures** (exact protocols unspecified but implied to support interoperability). **Validation and authentication** mechanisms are enforced at the DT Framework’s hub to ensure commands originate from authorized roles (e.g., *Facility Managers*) and comply with operational constraints. **Feedback systems** provide **confirmation of command execution** (e.g., acknowledgment signals, status updates) and **conflict resolution** via centralized arbitration, where conflicting commands (e.g., from overlapping control sources) are resolved based on priority rules or user-defined policies. The framework’s **runtime configuration management** allows dynamic adjustment of control logic without redeployment, ensuring adaptability to evolving physical constraints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation in the AECO domain is characterized by a **cyber-physical integration architecture** that orchestrates **static models** (e.g., BIM, CAD, or simulation models) with **runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensor feeds, operational logs, or environmental metrics) to create a **unified virtual representation** of physical assets. The orchestration relies on **carefully designed integration patterns**, though the document does not specify exact **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, service-oriented architecture, or edge computing). **Component integration** is implied to be **heterogeneous**, accommodating domain-specific tools (e.g., energy management systems, structural analysis software) and **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC, CityGML, or OPC UA) for seamless data exchange. **Resource management** is critical due to the **complexity of infrastructure environments**, but no details are provided on **load balancing**, **priority scheduling**, or **fault tolerance mechanisms**. **System-wide coordination** is inferred to require **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** to support predictive analytics and decision-making, though no explicit **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) or **temporal coordination protocols** (e.g., distributed locks, event sourcing) are mentioned."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans **five core phases**, each addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. **1. Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within the BIM environment, enriching it with semantic metadata for Building Control Systems (BCS) components. **2. Model Transformation**: Leveraging Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models, automating the generation of deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention. **3. Deployment**: DT artifacts are deployed, enabling real-time synchronization between physical infrastructure and the digital twin. **4. Operation**: Facility Managers interact with the twin for monitoring and control, while DT Engineers extend functionality via custom services. **5. Evolution**: The modular architecture supports runtime integration of new services, analytics, or operational adjustments, ensuring the DT adapts to changing requirements. **Transition Management**: The framework enforces seamless transitions between phases via standardized extension points and automated workflows, while **Lifecycle Governance** is implicit through role-based access (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for operations) and version-controlled artifact generation from BIM."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validity:**
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - High fidelity is implied through the integration of dynamic control logic and real-time data into static BIM models, ensuring the DT accurately reflects physical building operations.
    - The framework addresses BIM’s limitations by supporting the complete DT lifecycle, from device modeling to custom service development.

  - **Validation and Verification Methods:**
    - Not explicitly detailed, but the emphasis on automated transformation (MDE) and real-time synchronization suggests built-in validation during deployment and operation.
    - Practical insights from research projects (TwinLight, BOREALIS) likely inform validation processes.

  - **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification:**
    - Not specified, but potential sources may include sensor inaccuracies, model approximation errors, or delays in data transmission. Quantification methods are not described.

  - **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms:**
    - Implied through real-time data exchange and interactive visualization, allowing Facility Managers to identify discrepancies and correct them via the DT interface.
    - The modular architecture may support plug-and-play error correction services.

  - **Quality Assurance Processes:**
    - Ensured by the framework’s structured requirements (Table III) and lifecycle support, which mandate device modeling, real-time synchronization, and custom service integration.
    - Quality is maintained through standardized extension points and automated artifact generation.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the DT framework is designed to support **heterogeneous, interoperable communication** across distributed components, with a focus on **scalability, reliability, and low-latency data exchange**. The architecture employs a **modular, service-oriented topology**, where the DT Framework acts as a **central hub** coordinating data flows between **BIM tools** (used by *Building Control Engineers*), **IoT/OT devices** (physical sensors/actuators), and **facility management systems** (interfaced by *Facility Managers*). While specific **communication protocols** are not detailed, the system prioritizes **interoperability** (as per NF_X requirements) and likely integrates **industry-standard protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or proprietary BIM/IoT APIs) for seamless data exchange. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are optimized for real-time synchronization, with **redundancy mechanisms** (e.g., failover paths, replicated data streams) ensuring reliability. **Security protocols** are implied to include **authentication/authorization** (role-based access control for *DT Engineers*, *Facility Managers*, etc.) and **data encryption** during transmission, though exact implementations are not specified. The network supports **edge computing** for preprocessing and **cloud/on-premise hybrid deployment** to balance latency and scalability."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for the DT framework is **modular and scalable**, supporting **hybrid hosting models** (cloud, edge, and on-premise) to accommodate diverse operational needs. The system leverages **enriched BIM models** as the foundational digital asset, which are deployed and extended via the DT Framework’s **central hub**. **Computational requirements** are distributed across components, with **runtime configuration management** enabling dynamic resource allocation (e.g., scaling analytics services or control modules as demand fluctuates). **Storage requirements** are optimized for **low-overhead updates**, with data partitioned to balance performance and redundancy. The framework supports **incremental deployment models**, allowing **custom services and analytics** (integrated by *DT Engineers*) to be added without full system redeployment. **Availability and reliability** are assured through **redundant hosting** (e.g., cloud failover, edge caching) and **modular fault isolation**, ensuring continuous operation even during component failures. The architecture aligns with **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** for maintainability and accessibility, enabling users with varying technical expertise to interact with the DT via standardized interfaces (e.g., *Facility Managers* using monitoring dashboards)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration of the Digital Twin system in the AECO domain involves **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to enable seamless connectivity with **external systems**, including **enterprise resource planning (ERP) tools**, **building automation systems (BAS)**, **geospatial platforms**, and **other Digital Twins** operating within the same or adjacent domains. The document highlights the **fragmented nature of models and data** as a key challenge, necessitating adherence to **domain-specific standards** such as **Industry Foundation Classes (IFC)** for building information modeling, **CityGML** for urban infrastructure, or **OPC UA** for industrial IoT data. **Dependencies on external systems** are implied to include **real-time sensor networks**, **historical databases**, and **third-party analytics platforms**, though no specific **APIs**, **message brokers** (e.g., MQTT, AMQP), or **data serialization formats** (e.g., JSON, XML, Protocol Buffers) are referenced. Integration patterns may involve **event-driven architectures** or **service-oriented interfaces**, but the document does not specify **synchronization frequencies**, **data transformation pipelines**, or **security protocols** (e.g., OAuth, TLS) for cross-system communication. The **scope of integration** extends to **stakeholder collaboration** across the building lifecycle, from design to operations, but no technical details on **federated Digital Twin ecosystems** or **cross-organizational data governance** are provided."
]
